epigraph.text: Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom: and with all thy getting get understanding.
epigraph.quote: The method of maximum likelihood is the most important method of estimation in statistics.

content.text:
Maximum likelihood is perhaps the most important and widely used 
method of estimation.

content.title: Likelihood Function
content.content:
Given observed data $x_1, \ldots, x_n$, the **likelihood function** 
is:
\[
 L(\theta) = L(\theta; x_1, \ldots, x_n) = \prod_{i=1}^n f(x_i; \theta)
\]
where $f(x; \theta)$ is the PMF or PDF of a single observation.

content.text:
The likelihood measures how "likely" the observed data are, as a 
function of the parameter $\theta$.

content.title: Maximum Likelihood Estimator (MLE)
content.content:
The **maximum likelihood estimator** 
$\hat{\theta}_{MLE}$ is the value that maximizes $L(\theta)$:
\[
 \hat{\theta}_{MLE} = \arg\max_\theta L(\theta)
\]

content.text:
In practice, we maximize the **log-likelihood**:
\[
 \ell(\theta) = \ln L(\theta) = \sum_{i=1}^n \ln f(x_i; \theta)
\]
which is easier because sums are simpler than products.

content.title: MLE for Normal Mean
content.problem:
Find the MLE of $\mu$ from $X_1, \ldots, X_n \sim \Normal(\mu, \sigma^2)$ 
(with $\sigma^2$ known).
content.solution:
Log-likelihood:
\[
 \ell(\mu) = -\frac{n}{2}\ln(2\pi\sigma^2) - \frac{1}{2\sigma^2}\sum(x_i - \mu)^2
\]

Take derivative and set to zero:
\[
 \frac{d\ell}{d\mu} = \frac{1}{\sigma^2}\sum(x_i - \mu) = 0
\]

Solving: $\sum x_i = n\mu$, so $\hat{\mu}_{MLE} = \xbar$.

content.title: R.A. Fisher (1890--1962)
content.content:
Sir Ronald Fisher developed maximum likelihood estimation in the 1920s 
and proved many of its optimal properties. A deeply creative statistician, 
Fisher also made fundamental contributions to experimental design and 
population genetics. His work laid the foundations for modern statistics.

content.title: Properties of Maximum Likelihood Estimators
content.content:
Under regularity conditions:

1. MLEs are **consistent**

2. MLEs are **asymptotically normal**

3. MLEs are **asymptotically efficient** (achieve the 
 Cramér-Rao lower bound)

4. **Invariance:** If $\hat{\theta}$ is the MLE of $\theta$, 
 then $g(\hat{\theta})$ is the MLE of $g(\theta)$
content.label: Theorem
